mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls signal expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month increases coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates above 7 percent indicate significant slack in the labor market that suppresses wage growth and corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE > 7.0% is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates combined with flat or declining retail sales signal a liquidity trap where monetary stimulus fails to transmit to real demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with demand weakness
failure_condition: The joint interaction of FEDFUNDS and RSAFS achieves a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or positive returns dominate in low-rate, weak-sales regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during the early phase of a recovery from depressed capacity signals expanding corporate operating leverage and revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 60% of observations during recovery quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 700 thousand units signal durable construction strength and downstream demand for building materials and credit, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST > 700 is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth and subdued CPI inflation indicate a non-inflationary demand expansion that lifts profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 momentum and CPIAUCSL level produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate over a three-month window signals tightening labor market conditions that boost near-term consumer confidence and spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances where UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp over 3 months are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 97 signals manufacturing sector contraction that precedes broader corporate earnings declines, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO < 97 coincides with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.
